summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that uploading a ZIP file with path traversal entries (../../) successfully writes files outside the intended extraction directory. The PoC demonstrates this by extracting a proof file to /var/www/html/zipslip_proof.txt (one directory level above the extraction directory) and a webshell to /var/www/html/modules/zipslip_shell.php.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should extract the malicious ZIP file and create files in /var/www/html/ outside the /var/www/html/tmp/ extraction directory, demonstrating the path traversal vulnerability.
actual_observed_result: The reproduction script successfully built the Docker container, extracted the malicious ZIP, and confirmed the creation of /var/www/html/zipslip_proof.txt containing the proof message, and /var/www/html/modules/zipslip_shell.php containing a minimal webshell.
verification_method: The reproduction script checks for the existence of files outside the extraction directory (/var/www/html/zipslip_proof.txt and /var/www/html/modules/zipslip_shell.php) and verifies their content. The presence of these files confirms successful path traversal exploitation.
evidence_produced_by_run: Malicious ZIP file created with path traversal entries: ../../zipslip_proof.txt and ../../modules/zipslip_shell.php
ZIP file extracted to /var/www/html/tmp/ but files written to /var/www/html/ and /var/www/html/modules/
File /var/www/html/zipslip_proof.txt created successfully with timestamp proof
Webshell execution test confirmed PHP code execution capability

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh copies the target repository from the session directory to a temporary workspace and mounts it into the container at /var/www/html with read-write permissions.
reproduction.pinned_commit_or_ref: 2.4.17 (latest version based on update directory structure)
